avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow. Over a wide area wind slabs will form. In the course of the day the previously small wind slabs will increase in size once again. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1000 m and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Slab avalanches are possible. In addition the danger of avalanches will increase in the afternoon.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of the moderate to strong northeasterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. The wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. They are to be assessed with care and prudence.

tendency.highlights: In the afternoon danger level 3 (considerable) will be reached.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. The large wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant in all aspects above approximately 2000 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The current avalanche situation calls for careful route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before. This applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. The wind slabs have formed especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Snow sport activities outside marked and open pistes call for experience and great restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow of the weekend and the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes large wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant in all aspects at intermediate and high altitudes. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches and loose snow avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow will fall. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. In particular on steep slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant at intermediate and high altitudes.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for careful route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow. Up to 20 cm of snow will fall from early morning above approximately 1500 m. The easterly wind will transport the new snow significantly.
The new snow will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment: The wind will be strong at times. The wind slabs represent the main danger. They must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Moist avalanches are possible in some localities. Avalanches can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In all aspects the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather will be cloudy. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Little snow will fall in some localities. The sometimes strong wind will transport the snow. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. The sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant at intermediate and high altitudes. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for defensive route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. Over a wide area new snow is lying on a hard crust. Up to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, will fall from early morning. The easterly wind will transport the fresh and old snow.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh and older wind slabs are easy to recognise and to be assessed critically. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Fresh and older wind slabs are easy to recognise and can in some cases be released easily. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack over a wide area on very steep sunny slopes. On very steep sunny slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. As the day progresses, wet and gliding avalanches are possible.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size.
The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. This applies in particular on wind-loaded slopes above the tree line.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 50 cm of snow fell on Sunday above approximately 2000 m. The fresh snow and the sometimes deep wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Especially above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes:

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow. Over a wide area wind slabs will form. In the course of the day the previously small wind slabs will increase in size once again. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1000 m and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Slab avalanches are possible. In addition the danger of avalanches will increase in the afternoon.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of the moderate to strong northeasterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. The wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. They are to be assessed with care and prudence.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of the last two days are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. They are in some cases thick and can be released easily.
As a consequence of the southeasterly wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally since Wednesday.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: The wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant, in all aspects. Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 100 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Sunday above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions fostered a settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The weather conditions gave rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes.
Weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Significant decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops. The various wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily by a single winter sport participant in all aspects. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain above approximately 2000 m. The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase with altitude. Avalanches can reach medium size.
Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

Avalanches can in isolated cases be triggered in near-ground layers and reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.

Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from variable directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. As a consequence of a sometimes strong easterly wind, further wind slabs will form. Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the extensive wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Very steep, little used shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies at high altitude.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good in some case. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh wind slabs are easy to recognise and can in some cases be released easily especially at their margins. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs are easy to recognise are to be assessed critically. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack over a wide area on very steep sunny slopes.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.
The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 25 cm of snow fell on Sunday. The fresh snow and the wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Especially above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes:

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released easily.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes deep wind slabs can be released easily in particular on northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach large size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 1700 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Below approximately 1800 m: The old snowpack remains moist.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size moderately. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Avalanches can in isolated cases be triggered in near-ground layers and reach medium size. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The wind will be strong at times. As a consequence of the easterly wind the wind slabs will increase in size moderately. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on west to north to east facing aspects at elevated altitudes.

West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities.
The wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released easily. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs of the last two days are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. They can in some cases be released easily.
As a consequence of the southeasterly wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally since Wednesday.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases in all aspects.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow fell on Sunday above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions facilitated a settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Sunshine and high temperatures gave rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2700 m.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Significant decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops. The various wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily in some places, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase with altitude. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can be triggered in near-ground layers. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow has fallen since Monday. As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from variable directions, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed. Fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow. Over a wide area wind slabs will form. In the course of the day the previously small wind slabs will increase in size once again.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of the moderate to strong northeasterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. The wind slabs represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day individual moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.
Dry avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: New snow is lying on a moist old snowpack. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.
